ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Dec-19-2025 Discharged
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for an Order of Examination as to Judgment Debtor Richard Kok Wai Chang

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505; Matter reported by Certified Shorthand Reporter Peggy Tsujimoto #5229.

Judgment Creditor Suae-Chen Chang is present represented by Edwin Lamar Bradley.
Judgment Debtor Richard Kok Wai Chang is present.

Mr. Chang is sworn. Order of Examination began and is concluded. This matter is discharged.

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Oct-31-2025 Continued For Letter To Dec-19-25 At 11:00 A.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar for the Judgment Creditor Suae-Chen Chang's request for an Order of Examination as to Judgment Debtor Richard Kok Wai Chang.

Judge Dennis Hayashi; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505 - Matter not reported.

Judgment Creditor Suae-Chen Chang is present represented by Edwin Lamar Bradley.
Judgment Creditor's Court Reported Lydia Zinn 223 is present.
Judgment Debtor Richard Kok Wai Chang is not present.

A valid proof of service is on file.

The court issues a bench letter warning of a possible warrent being issued if there is no appearance at the next calling of this matter.

This matter is continued to December 19, 2025 at 11:00am in dept. 505.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Attorney; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-20-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS SUAE-CHEN CHANG and HELEN CHANG's Motion To Substitute Attorney.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Suae-Chen Chang and Helen Han Chang's unopposed motion to substitute attorney is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2003 Off Calendar

Default Prove Up In Scd.
Off Calendar Sep-11-2003 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for Default Prove Up hearing this day.
Commissioner Sue Kaplan Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: JoAnn Prior.
Appearances:
Herbert Yanowitz, Esq. for Plaintiff Suae-Chen Chang and Helen Han Chang, parties present with Mandarin Interpreter, Bei Bei Song, were sworn & examined.
Plaintiff's Exh. #1 - Copy of check #121 dated 4-29-01 --------- ID/EVID.
Exh. #2 - Copy of C/C #308-761002810 dated 4-25-01---ID/EVID.
Exh. #3 - Copy of Note dated 4-25-01 ----------------ID/EVID.
After hearing the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the plaintiffs and against the defendants as follows:
Principal of $100,000.00.
Interest of $23,808.60.
Attorney's fees of $3,500.00.
Costs of $349.50.
For the total of $127,658.10.
Judgment signed in open court.
Counsel stipulated for return of exhibits in open court.
